compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Why

`server.json` is the MCP registry front-door contract, but it has drifted from
the real config surface. It lists only `REBAR_ROOT`, `REBAR_MCP_READONLY`, and
the **deprecated** `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_RECONCILE_LIVE`, while the actual gates are
`mcp.allow_llm` (`REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM`) and `mcp.allow_jira_sync`
(`REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_JIRA_SYNC`). Users see the LLM/reconcile tools appear but fail
at call time with no manifest hint, and new installs inherit deprecated
terminology. Nothing prevents this from re-drifting.

## What

- Update `server.json` `environmentVariables` to the current contract: add
  `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM` and `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_JIRA_SYNC` with accurate
  descriptions; keep `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_RECONCILE_LIVE` only if explicitly labeled
  a deprecated alias (prefer replacing it with the current name).
- Add a **CI drift-guard** that compares the manifest's advertised env-var set
  against the canonical gate list in code (the `REBAR_MCP_*` env gates read in
  `mcp_server.py` / the readonly+allow gates), failing the build on divergence —
  mirroring the existing prompt-index / criteria-routing drift gates in
  `.github/workflows/test.yml`.

## Scope

`server.json` + one CI gate (a small script under `scripts/` or a step in
`.github/workflows/test.yml`). Identify the single source of truth for the env
gates so the guard has an authoritative list to diff against. No behavior change
to the server itself.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `server.json` advertises `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM` and
  `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_JIRA_SYNC` with correct descriptions.
- [ ] The deprecated `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_RECONCILE_LIVE` is removed or explicitly
  marked as a deprecated alias.
- [ ] A CI gate fails when `server.json`'s env-var set diverges from the
  canonical `REBAR_MCP_*` gate list in code.
- [ ] The gate passes on the corrected manifest; `make lint` stays green.
compiled_state.title: Fix server.json MCP env/config contract and add a CI drift-guard
